39 ## [10/16] Notes: “[Session 1] Sharing on SLO updates” Apr 7, 2026
40 Source: gmail | Thread: 19d67245db53ca1b | Relevance: 7/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-07T08:52:00+00:00 | Labels: slo-transition, alerting-strategy, reliability-metrics, datadog-ops, architecture-governance | Senders: Gemini
41 This email contains auto-generated meeting notes from a session on April 7, 2026, regarding the transition to SLO V2. The discussion highlights that SLO V1 was ineffective due to false positives and maintenance overhead. SLO V2 introduces a request-based formula for better customer impact reflection and dynamic target setting. Key action items include enabling V2 alerts in Datadog (fast/slow burn, error budget) and muting all V1 monitors by tomorrow. The group must immediately acknowledge new alerts and investigate root causes. Additionally, the team plans to review and completely remove the SLO V1 system within 3-4 weeks after a month of observation. As Michael Bui owns backend architecture standards and SLO governance, this strategic update on engineering metrics directly impacts his domain.

43 ## [11/16] [RAW Overdue] Expired Risk Acceptance & Waiver Form
44 Source: gmail | Thread: 19d65769960dcea9 | Relevance: 7/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-07T09:02:00+08:00 | Labels: compliance-alert, risk-waiver, access-management, automation-reminder, security-governance | Senders: cyberrisk.automation | Work Items: RAW-20240306_01-v1.0
45 Automated reminder regarding an expired Risk Acceptance & Waiver (RAW-20240306_01-v1.0) for the 'Signcloud SaaS User access management' asset at FPG-Fairprice. The waiver expired on May 15, 2025, due to a lack of user access management by the business owner. Michael Bui is explicitly listed in the recipient field (Direct mention). If residual risks remain unremediated, a new RAW form must be submitted via Gsuite/Google Docs immediately. The process requires Section A completion, review by Cybersecurity Risk Team, and sign-off from Technology Governance and Compliance. Failure to remediate or submit updates will result in continued automated reminders.

47 ## [12/16] Issue with the event segment audience pipeline.
48 Source: gmail | Thread: 19ce6ebdc3530d4d | Relevance: 9/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-07T09:35:00+00:00 | Labels: event-flooding, scale-lag, vendor-escalation, architect-review, sync-offline | Senders: Vipul, me | People: Vipul Gupta, Michael Bui, Nabhey Samant, Kunal Parekh
49 Vipul Gupta (Osmos) reported recurring event flooding issues in the segment audience pipeline. Despite Michael explaining that the initial March 13 spike to 14M events was a planned sync for 'popup_myinfo_unverified', Vipul confirmed autoscaling lag caused 5XX errors due to sudden load surges. The core issue persists: attaching new audiences triggers single-batch data floods, overwhelming real-time processing and preventing effective scaling. Vipul requested immediate coordination with the Segment team to implement ramp-up controls. As a fallback, Osmos proposed shifting from real-time sync to offline batch processing via GCS if no immediate fix is found. This requires Michael's technical judgment to evaluate architectural changes (real-time vs. offline) and his leadership input to engage vendor/stakeholder discussions on platform scalability limits. The issue directly impacts production reliability and engineering metrics governed by Michael.
50 
51 ## [13/16] [JIRA] (DPD-838) Transition to Impression-Based Inventory & Multi-Banner Delivery
52 Source: gmail | Thread: 19d2e82fa82f66fe | Relevance: 9/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-08T01:57:00+00:00 | Labels: ad-tech-architecture, inventory-transition, osmos-integration, multi-banner-delivery, engineering-governance | Senders: Nikhil | Work Items: DPD-838 | People: Chee Hoe Leong, Nikhil Grover, Michael Bui, Milind Badame
53 Michael Bui is an active participant in Jira ticket DPD-838 concerning the transition to impression-based inventory and multi-banner delivery. The discussion involves architecting a shift from fixed-slot tenancy to a system integrated with OSMOS. In a recent update on April 8th, Chee Hoe Leong clarified implementation details for preprod setup, confirming that multiple ads can be served per ad unit (AU) with logic prioritizing endemic content and deduplicating within slots. The conversation addressed edge cases involving duplicate AU keys (e.g., OG_HOMEPAGE_BIG_1 vs _BIG_2) and confirmed that all mapped positions are routed to OSMOS based on source sheet settings. Previously, Michael identified critical ambiguities regarding video support limits, non-endemic logic, and capacity constraints. The ticket has progressed from 'To Be Defined' to 'In Development', with Milind Badame noting the necessity for manual E2E testing due to complex visual scenarios. This thread represents a direct architectural review requiring Michael's technical judgment on scope and fallback behaviors.
54 
55 ## [14/16] Re: Reminder:2026-OC-02477 - 349-SENGKANG GRAND MALL
56 Source: gmail | Thread: 19d6aea0617f1492 | Relevance: 9/10 | Updated: 2026-04-08T02:26:00+00:00 | Labels: promo-troubleshooting, catalog-issue, b1g1-promotion, product-catalog, sengg-kang-mall | Senders: David Anura Cooray | Work Items: 2026-OC-02477 | People: David Anura Cooray, Daryl
57 David Anura Cooray directly requested assistance regarding a missing B1G1 free promotion for a specific SKU on the S&G platform at 349-Sengkang Grand Mall. The request seeks confirmation that the offer is configured as a 'unity complex promo.' This issue falls under Michael Bui's Product Engineering mandate, specifically concerning e-commerce capabilities and product catalog/promotion logic. As an active production inquiry requiring technical verification of promo setup, it demands immediate engineering attention to ensure correct customer-facing behavior.
58 
59 ## [15/16] Issue with Missing UBID
60 Source: gmail | Thread: 19d52fdf13c8b0bd | Relevance: 7/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-08T04:24:00+00:00 | Labels: retail-media, data-integrity, vendor-escalation, architecture-fix, tracking-error | Senders: Rahul, me | People: Rahul Jain, Vipul Gupta, Aman Khatri, Michael Bui, Nikhil Grover
61 On April 3, 2026, vendor partner Rahul Jain (Osmos) flagged a critical data integrity issue regarding missing User Browsing IDs (UBID) in the retail media pipeline. Michael Bui previously responded by requesting clarification on UBID/uclid definitions and impact scope. On April 8, Jain escalated this to Vipul Gupta and Aman Khatri, explicitly instructing them to share necessary information with Michael and Nikhil Grover regarding the specific ad units involved. This represents a direct technical escalation requiring immediate architectural review and configuration correction to restore SLO compliance for impression tracking.
62 
63 ## [16/16] SLO Version 2 Alerts Now Live — Smarter Alerting, Less Noise, Better Visibility
64 Source: gmail | Thread: 19d6b5b1cca61095 | Relevance: 7/10 | Updated: 2026-04-08T04:30:00+00:00 | Labels: slo-migration, alerting-update, reliability-engineering, ops-improvement, datadog-changes | Senders: Gopi Krishna Dodla | People: Gopi Krishna Dodla
65 Reliability Engineering has migrated SLO monitoring from V1 (Monitor-based) to V2 (Metric-based). This change disables old alerts and enables new burn rate alerts in Datadog, reducing false positives and improving incident detection. The migration was announced by Gopi Krishna Dodla on April 8, 2026. Michael's teams must monitor the new alert routing for one week to ensure correctness. While no immediate action is required, this update directly impacts the engineering governance standards Michael oversees and affects the SLO targets he enforces.
